programme.title: FANTASY SHOW FRI 04 APR
programme.sub-title: Saka or Salah? Foden or Fernandes? Wilson or Watkins? Picking your Fantasy team is never easy but this season the Fantasy Show is on hand to help you with those big decisions. Fantasy experts will crunch the numbers and give their opinion on which Premier League stars you should be selecting ahead of each FPL Gameweek.

programme.title: FANZONE FRI 04 APR
programme.sub-title: This is the show that puts international fans at the heart of the Premier League conversation. A phone-in for the digital generation, fans from around the world join our studio discussion via camera phones and weblinks to have their say about their teams and their favourite players.

A phone-in for the digital generation, fans from around the world join our studio discussion via camera phones and weblinks to have their say about their teams and their favourite players.

programme.title: PREVIEW FRI 04 APR
programme.sub-title: In this week’s Preview Show, Carl Anka and Ian Irving break down the Manchester Derby ahead of a huge game at Old Trafford. Elsewhere, we look ahead to another rivalry game as Crystal Palace host Brighton, and Lee Hendrie previews the meeting between Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest as the race for Europe heats up.
programme.desc: In this week’s Preview Show, Carl Anka and Ian Irving break down the Manchester Derby ahead of a huge game at Old Trafford. Elsewhere, we look ahead to another rivalry game as Crystal Palace host Brighton, and Lee Hendrie previews the meeting between Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest as the race for Europe heats up.

programme.title: WELCOME TO THE WEEKEND FRI 04 APR
programme.sub-title: Light-hearted, inclusive and entertaining, Welcome to the Weekend is the show that kicks off the Premier League weekend. We will focus on the football but also on the lifestyle topics that footballers and fans love - whether it’s food and family, music and movies, or fashion and gaming. The On-Screen Team will bring chemistry, energy and a load of fun.
programme.desc: Light-hearted, inclusive and entertaining, Welcome to the Weekend is the show that kicks off the Premier League weekend. We will focus on the football but also on the lifestyle topics that footballers and fans love - whether it’s food and family, music and movies, or fashion and gaming. The On-Screen Team will bring chemistry, energy and a load of fun.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP34: ANDONI IRAOLA
programme.sub-title: Having arrived on these shores to manage Bournemouth in 2023, Andoni Iraola took over a club which had narrowly avoided relegation. Today, they are considered one of the toughest sides to play against in the Premier League. With exclusive access, this week the Bournemouth manager gives the Big Interview a guided tour of some of the local spots he likes to visit on the Dorset coastline. Iraola opens up on growing up in Spain, playing alongside Xabi Alonso and Mikel Arteta, how his footballing philosophy is turning Bournemouth’s fortunes around and what he loves most about the place he now calls home.
programme.desc: Having arrived on these shores to manage Bournemouth in 2023, Andoni Iraola took over a club which had narrowly avoided relegation. Today, they are considered one of the toughest sides to play against in the Premier League. With exclusive access, this week the Bournemouth manager gives the Big Interview a guided tour of some of the local spots he likes to visit on the Dorset coastline. Iraola opens up on growing up in Spain, playing alongside Xabi Alonso and Mikel Arteta, how his footballing philosophy is turning Bournemouth’s fortunes around and what he loves most about the place he now calls home.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP34: ANDONI IRAOLA
programme.sub-title: Having arrived on these shores to manage Bournemouth in 2023, Andoni Iraola took over a club which had narrowly avoided relegation. Today, they are considered one of the toughest sides to play against in the Premier League. With exclusive access, this week the Bournemouth manager gives the Big Interview a guided tour of some of the local spots he likes to visit on the Dorset coastline. Iraola opens up on growing up in Spain, playing alongside Xabi Alonso and Mikel Arteta, how his footballing philosophy is turning Bournemouth’s fortunes around and what he loves most about the place he now calls home.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP20: COLE PALMER
programme.sub-title: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. Considered one of the most technically gifted players the Premier League has ever seen, Gianfranco Zola visits the Chelsea training ground to talk to the man currently lighting up the Premier League, Cole Palmer, about various aspects of his game. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP28: (Subbed) MANUEL UGARTE
programme.sub-title: From a city where football is drummed into you at a young age, hard work and tenacity becomes part of your game; Manchester United’s Manuel Ugarte is no different. The Uruguayan shares stories of growing up in Montevideo, how he’s settling into life in Manchester, currently in his second spell under manager Ruben Amorim. With an extensive collection of players' shirts from those he has faced over the years, Ugarte has brought along some of his favourites to share the stories behind them.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP45: TITLE RUN IN: TRUE TURNING POINTS
programme.sub-title: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP33: ANDY COLE
programme.sub-title: From leaving Arsenal as a promising, young striker, to lifting the Champions League with Manchester United, Andrew Cole discusses his incredible footballing journey in this week’s Big Interview. Cole opens up about being noticed by Sir Alex Ferguson after scoring against his side, winning the treble in 1999, his telepathic understanding with Dwight Yorke as well as his resolve in dealing with recent health concerns.
programme.desc: From leaving Arsenal as a promising, young striker, to lifting the Champions League with Manchester United, Andrew Cole discusses his incredible footballing journey in this week’s Big Interview. Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.
programme.desc: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
